core_mechanism: Tomographic inversion: reconstructing a spatially-resolved local field from a set of line-of-sight-integrated (projected) measurements over an inhomogeneous sample, so that a locally-homogeneous response can be recovered from spatially-averaged data
brief_reason: The abstract obtains the spatial distribution and the 'homogeneous' local spectrum of a spatially inhomogeneous (trapped) system via in situ imaging plus 3D image reconstruction, which is exactly a projection-to-local-field tomographic inversion (here paired with a frequency-swept resonant response probe that exposes the spectral gap).
